tasks.title: Create Configuration File Structure and Loading Utilities
tasks.description: Set up config directory with fastagent.config.yaml, fastagent.secrets.yaml (gitignored), and approval_rules.yaml templates with environment variable substitution support.
tasks.details:
Create config/ directory. Use PyYAML 6.0.2 for parsing. Implement config loader with python-dotenv 1.0.1 for env var substitution (${VAR_NAME}). Add validation with pydantic 2.9.2. Template fastagent.config.yaml with MCP servers list, model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', max_tokens=4096. fastagent.secrets.yaml with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_TOKEN placeholders. approval_rules.yaml with tool_name: true/false mappings. Add .gitignore for secrets. Pseudo-code: ```python
def load_config(path): with open(path) as f: data = yaml.safe_load(f) return substitute_env_vars(data)```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: verify YAML parsing, env substitution (dotenv), validation passes/fails, defaults applied. Mock env vars, assert loaded dict structure matches schema. Coverage: 90%+.

tasks.title: Implement PlatformInterface Abstract Base Class
tasks.description: Create connectors/interface.py with PlatformInterface ABC and PlatformMessage dataclass for standardized message handling.
tasks.details:
Use abc module and dataclasses. Define PlatformMessage with thread_id:str, user_id:str, content:str, platform:str, channel_id:str, is_mention:bool. Abstract methods: async receive_message(event), async send_message(thread_id, content, msg_type), async request_approval(thread_id, tool_name, params, timeout), async handle_button_click(payload). Use typing annotations. Pseudo-code: ```python
class PlatformInterface(ABC): @abstractmethod async def send_message(self, ...): pass``` Export PlatformMessage, PlatformInterface.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: verify ABC cannot instantiate, dataclass serialization/deserialization, type checking. Mock subclass implementation tests.

tasks.title: Install and Verify fast-agent-mcp Framework Integration
tasks.description: Install fast-agent-mcp and create minimal POC agent with MCP server connection and basic tool execution.
tasks.details: pip install fast-agent-mcp==latest (pin to 0.2.5+ as of 2026), pydantic, anthropic==0.44.0. Create test_agent.py with @fast.agent(model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', mcp_servers=[{'url': 'ws://mcp-server1'}]). Test agent.send('test message'). Verify tool discovery from 5-6 MCP servers. POC: minimal ToolRunnerHook that logs tool calls. Confirm async hook support and exception blocking.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: agent instantiation, MCP connection (mock servers), tool discovery (assert 90+ tools), basic message processing, hook interception. Verify no tool calls without approval simulation.

tasks.title: Implement ApprovalRulesManager
tasks.description: Create approval/rules_manager.py to load and query tool approval rules from approval_rules.yaml.
tasks.details:
Use PyYAML 6.0.2, pydantic for validation. Class ApprovalRulesManager with load_rules(path), requires_approval(tool_name) -> bool (default False). Support tool categories: {'filesystem/*': true}. Cache loaded rules. Pseudo-code: ```python
class ApprovalRulesManager: def __init__(self, config_path): self.rules = self._load_yaml(config_path) def requires_approval(self, tool): return self.rules.get(tool, False)```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: rule loading, exact match, category match (glob 'filesystem/read'), missing tool default False, invalid YAML graceful fallback, cache hit/miss.

tasks.title: Implement ApprovalTracker System
tasks.description: Create approval/approval_tracker.py for tracking pending approvals with async resolution and timeouts.
tasks.details: Use asyncio.Future, uuid for approval_id. Dict[approval_id, ToolApprovalRequest]. Methods: create_approval(thread_id, tool_name, params, timeout=300) -> approval_id, resolve_approval(approval_id, approved:bool), get_future(approval_id) -> Future[bool]. Timeout via asyncio.wait_for. Cleanup expired. Dataclass ToolApprovalRequest(approval_id, thread_id, tool_name, tool_input:dict, tool_use_id:str, created_at:datetime, future:Future).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: create/track/resolve, concurrent approvals (asyncio.gather), timeout deny, cleanup, invalid ID handling. Mock asyncio for deterministic timing.

subtasks.title: Implement Dataclass and Storage Structure
subtasks.description: Define ToolApprovalRequest dataclass and initialize storage dict mapping approval_id to ToolApprovalRequest instances using asyncio.Future.
subtasks.details: Create @dataclass ToolApprovalRequest with fields: approval_id:UUID, thread_id:str, tool_name:str, tool_input:dict, tool_use_id:str, created_at:datetime, future:asyncio.Future[bool]. Implement self._approvals: Dict[UUID, ToolApprovalRequest] in ApprovalTracker class with proper type hints and imports (uuid, asyncio, datetime).

subtasks.title: Implement Core Methods with Timeout Handling
subtasks.description: Add create_approval, resolve_approval, get_future methods with timeout support via asyncio.wait_for.
subtasks.details: Implement create_approval(thread_id, tool_name, params, timeout=300) -> UUID: generate UUID, create Future, store ToolApprovalRequest, return id. resolve_approval(approval_id, approved:bool): set future.result(approved). get_future(approval_id) -> Future[bool]: retrieve or raise KeyError. Ensure thread-safe access.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for create/track/resolve, concurrent approvals with asyncio.gather, timeout deny using asyncio.wait_for

subtasks.title: Implement Cleanup Mechanism and Unit Tests
subtasks.description: Add expired approval cleanup and comprehensive unit tests for concurrent access and edge cases.
subtasks.details: Implement periodic cleanup task removing expired approvals (created_at + timeout). Use asyncio.Lock for concurrent access protection. Write tests: concurrent approvals (asyncio.gather), timeout deny, cleanup, invalid ID handling, race condition prevention. Mock asyncio for deterministic timing.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: concurrent approvals (asyncio.gather), timeout deny via asyncio.wait_for, cleanup expired, invalid ID handling, mock asyncio for deterministic timing

tasks.title: Create agent/agent_config.py and ChatPlatformToolAgent
tasks.description: Define fast-agent with MCP config and custom ChatPlatformToolAgent using ToolRunnerHooks for approval integration.
tasks.details: agent/agent_config.py: def create_agent() -> ToolAgent. Use @fast.agent from config. agent/tool_agent.py: class ChatPlatformToolAgent(ToolAgent, ToolRunnerHookCapable): async def before_tool_call(self, tool_use_id, tool_name, tool_input): if rules.requires(tool_name): approval_id = tracker.create(...); approved = await tracker.get_future(approval_id); if not approved: raise ToolApprovalDenied(). Integrate platform_interface.send_status().
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: agent creation, MCP tool discovery, hook interception (mock platform/rules/tracker), approval block via exception, result reporting.

subtasks.title: Implement agent_config.py factory function
subtasks.description: Create agent/agent_config.py with def create_agent() -> ToolAgent using @fast.agent decorator and MCP configuration from loaded config.
subtasks.details: Use fast-agent framework with @fast.agent decorator. Load MCP config from config/fastagent.config.yaml including model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', max_tokens=4096, and MCP servers list. Return configured ToolAgent instance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: verify agent creation succeeds, MCP config loaded correctly, agent type is ToolAgent.

subtasks.title: Define ChatPlatformToolAgent base class
subtasks.description: Create agent/tool_agent.py with class ChatPlatformToolAgent(ToolAgent, ToolRunnerHookCapable) integrating platform_interface for status updates.
subtasks.details: Inherit from ToolAgent and ToolRunnerHookCapable. Initialize platform_interface in __init__. Add async methods for status messaging via platform_interface.send_status(). Ensure hook compatibility.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: instantiation, inheritance verification, platform_interface integration mock.

subtasks.title: Implement before_tool_call approval workflow
subtasks.description: Add async def before_tool_call(self, tool_use_id, tool_name, tool_input) with rules check, tracker approval, and exception on denial.
subtasks.details: Check if rules.requires(tool_name), create approval_id = tracker.create(...), await approved = tracker.get_future(approval_id), raise ToolApprovalDenied() if not approved. Call platform_interface.send_status() for updates.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: mock rules/tracker/platform, verify hook interception, approval flow (approve/deny), exception raised on denial.

subtasks.title: Add Custom ToolApprovalDenied exception and tests
subtasks.description: Define custom ToolApprovalDenied exception class and integrate status messaging. Update tests for full agent workflow.
subtasks.details: class ToolApprovalDenied(Exception): pass. Ensure exception carries tool_name and reason. Add platform_interface.send_status('approval_denied') in workflow. Include in agent test suite.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test exception raising/handling, integration test full workflow block via exception, coverage for deny path.

tasks.title: Implement SlackConnector
tasks.description: Create connectors/slack_connector.py implementing PlatformInterface for Slack events, messages, approvals, buttons.
tasks.details:
slack-bolt 1.19.2. Handle app_mention, message, block_actions. Normalize to PlatformMessage (event['ts'] as thread_id). send_message: say() with blocks/embeds. request_approval: post ephemeral with buttons (approve/deny actions). handle_button_click: ack(), update tracker. Integrate factoids.check_message(). Pseudo-code: ```python
class SlackConnector(PlatformInterface): async def handle_event(self, body, say): msg = PlatformMessage(...) if factoids.match(msg): await say(factoids.response); return await self.agent.process(msg)```
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: mock Slack app, mention→agent→response, approval buttons→resolve, factoid bypass, thread handling, API errors (429/500). Verify no regressions.

subtasks.title: Slack app setup with slack-bolt
subtasks.description: Initialize SlackConnector class inheriting PlatformInterface, install slack-bolt 1.19.2, configure App with bot/app tokens, enable SocketModeHandler for event listening.
subtasks.details: Create connectors/slack_connector.py with AsyncApp(token=SLACK_BOT_TOKEN), SocketModeHandler setup. Load env vars securely. Verify app starts without errors using basic logger[1][2].

subtasks.title: Implement event handlers for app_mention and block_actions
subtasks.description: Add @app.event('app_mention') and @app.action for block_actions handling incoming Slack events with ack(), say(), and logger integration.
subtasks.details: In handle_event: parse body, ack actions immediately, handle app_mention by normalizing message and routing to agent.process or factoids[1][2]. Use async say() for responses.

subtasks.title: PlatformMessage normalization logic
subtasks.description: Create normalization function to convert Slack event dict to PlatformMessage instance, mapping event['ts'] to thread_id, user, text, channel details.
subtasks.details: Def normalize_slack_message(event): return PlatformMessage(thread_id=event['ts'], content=event['text'], user_id=event['user'], channel=event['channel']). Handle subtypes[1].

subtasks.title: Approval button workflows implementation
subtasks.description: Implement request_approval with ephemeral postephemeral containing approve/deny buttons, handle_button_click with ack() and tracker resolution.
subtasks.details: Use blocks with actions: action_id='approve', 'deny'. In callback: ack(), resolve_approval(approval_id, approved=True/False) via ApprovalTracker. Integrate uuid for IDs[1].

subtasks.title: Integration tests with mocked Slack API
subtasks.description: Write pytest integration tests mocking Slack app, covering mention→agent→response, approval→resolve, factoids bypass, thread handling, API errors (429/500).
subtasks.details: Use pytest-asyncio, unittest.mock for App/SocketModeHandler. Assert say() calls, tracker updates, no regressions. Test rate limits with side_effects[1][2].
subtasks.testStrategy: Run pytest with coverage >90%, verify mocks match real flows

tasks.title: Implement DiscordConnector
tasks.description: Create connectors/discord_connector.py implementing PlatformInterface for Discord messages, approvals, interactions.
tasks.details: discord.py 2.4.0 (nextcord fork for stability). on_message, on_interaction. Normalize PlatformMessage (message.id for thread_id if threaded). send_message: embeds. request_approval: send with View (Approve/Deny buttons, custom_id=approval_id). handle interaction: defer(), update tracker. Factoid integration. Pseudo-code: similar to Slack but DiscordClient.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: mock Discord client, message→agent→embed response, approval View→resolve, mention detection, DM vs channel, API errors.

subtasks.title: Set up DiscordClient with nextcord
subtasks.description: Implement DiscordClient class inheriting from PlatformInterface using nextcord 2.4.0, configure intents for messages and interactions, load bot token from environment.
subtasks.details: Use nextcord.Client or commands.Bot with intents.message_content=True and intents.guilds=True. Implement __init__ with bot setup, run method. Handle on_ready event. Reference nextcord quickstart patterns.

subtasks.title: Implement on_message and on_interaction event handlers
subtasks.description: Create async event handlers for on_message and on_interaction events, normalize incoming Discord messages to PlatformMessage, integrate factoids.check_message(), defer interactions.
subtasks.details: In on_message: extract message.id as thread_id if threaded, detect mentions/DMs vs channels. In on_interaction: handle View button clicks, defer() then process approval_id. Similar to SlackConnector pattern.

subtasks.title: Develop PlatformMessage normalization with thread handling
subtasks.description: Create normalization logic converting Discord Message/Interaction to PlatformMessage, handle thread_id using message.id for threaded messages, support DM/channel logic and mention detection.
subtasks.details: PlatformMessage fields: thread_id=message.id if message.thread else channel.id, user_id=author.id, content=message.content. Distinguish DM (type==DMChannel) vs guild channels.

subtasks.title: Build ApprovalView components for request_approval
subtasks.description: Implement Discord View class with Approve/Deny buttons using custom_id=approval_id, integrate with ApprovalTracker for send_message with embeds and handle interaction updates.
subtasks.details: request_approval: create View(ApprovalView(approval_id)), send embed with tool details. View.callback: defer(), resolve via tracker.resolve_approval(approved). Timeout handling via tracker.

subtasks.title: Create mocked integration tests for DiscordConnector
subtasks.description: Write integration tests mocking Discord client, verify message→agent→embed response flow, approval View→resolve, mention detection, DM vs channel, factoid integration, API error handling.
subtasks.details: Use pytest-asyncio and unittest.mock. Mock nextcord.Message, Interaction, Client. Test flows: factoid bypass, approval timeout deny, thread handling, 429/500 error resilience. No regressions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run pytest with coverage >90%, verify async flows with asyncio.gather, mock tracker futures.

tasks.title: Update app.py and Integrate Factoids with Multi-Platform Support
tasks.description: Refactor main app.py to initialize connectors/agent, handle multiple platforms. Update factoids.py for PlatformMessage.
tasks.details: app.py: async def main(): config = load_config(); slack = SlackConnector(config); discord = DiscordConnector(config); agent = create_agent(); slack.agent = discord.agent = agent; app.start(). Factoids: class FactoidManager with check(msg: PlatformMessage) -> str|None, 30s cooldown (redis or in-memory), random response. Multi-platform event loop.
tasks.testStrategy: E2E tests: parallel Slack/Discord messages, factoid hit/miss/cooldown, agent processing isolation, multi-thread conversations. Load testing 10 concurrent.

subtasks.title: Refactor app.py for Multi-Platform Initialization
subtasks.description: Update app.py to load config, initialize SlackConnector and DiscordConnector, create shared agent, assign agent to connectors, and start multi-platform event loop.
subtasks.details: Implement async def main(): with config=load_config(), slack=SlackConnector(config), discord=DiscordConnector(config), agent=create_agent(), shared assignment slack.agent = discord.agent = agent, and app.start() for event loop coordination. Ensure async compatibility across platforms.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for connector init, agent sharing, config loading; integration test for startup without errors.

subtasks.title: Implement FactoidManager with Cooldown Logic
subtasks.description: Create FactoidManager class in factoids.py that processes PlatformMessage, checks 30s cooldown using in-memory or Redis, and returns random factoid response or None.
subtasks.details: Define class FactoidManager with async def check(self, msg: PlatformMessage) -> str|None. Use dict/timestamp for in-memory cooldown or Redis for persistence. Select random response from predefined factoids on cooldown miss. Thread-safe for multi-platform.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: cooldown hit/miss, random selection, multi-thread access; mock PlatformMessage and Redis.

subtasks.title: Add E2E Tests for Multi-Platform and Factoids
subtasks.description: Develop end-to-end tests verifying parallel Slack/Discord message processing, factoid triggers/cooldowns, shared agent isolation, and concurrent conversations.
subtasks.details: Use pytest-asyncio for E2E: simulate parallel messages on both platforms, assert factoid responses with cooldown, verify agent processes independently, test 10 concurrent loads. Mock connectors and agent for isolation.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E test suite: parallel platform messages, factoid hit/miss/cooldown scenarios, agent isolation, load testing with 10 concurrent sessions.

tasks.title: Implement Comprehensive Test Suite, Error Handling, and Documentation
tasks.description: Create tests/unit, tests/integration, tests/e2e with 80%+ coverage. Add error handling and docs.
tasks.details: pytest 8.3.3, pytest-asyncio 0.24.0, pytest-cov. Mock external APIs (responses, aioresponses 0.7.6). Error handling: try/except in hooks/connectors, user-friendly msgs (no stacktraces), structlog 24.4.0 logging. Docs: README with setup, architecture.md from PRD. Coverage: unit 70%, integration 20%, e2e 10%.
tasks.testStrategy: Run pytest --cov=bot --cov-report=html. Verify 80% line/75% branch coverage. Test error scenarios (API fail, timeout, invalid input), recovery continues operation.

subtasks.title: Unit Test Structure Setup
subtasks.description: Create tests/unit directory with pytest-asyncio fixtures and test files for core modules like approval_tracker, rules_manager, hooks.
subtasks.details: Use pytest 8.3.3, pytest-asyncio 0.24.0. Create conftest.py with async fixtures (mock asyncio, dataclass instances). Target 70% unit coverage for Tasks 4,5. Structure: tests/unit/test_approval/, test_rules/, test_hooks/.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run pytest tests/unit/ --cov=approval --cov=rules --cov-report=term-missing. Verify 70%+ coverage, no failures.

subtasks.title: Integration Test Mocking Strategy
subtasks.description: Implement tests/integration with mocking for SlackConnector (Task7), DiscordConnector (Task8) using responses, aioresponses 0.7.6.
subtasks.details: Mock external APIs (Slack/Discord endpoints, MCP servers). Test connector.handle_event → agent → response flows, factoid integration, API errors (429/500). Use pytest.mark.asyncio for async tests.
subtasks.testStrategy: pytest tests/integration/ -v. Verify mock calls match expected (say/postMessage), no real API calls, approval resolution.

subtasks.title: E2E Test Scenarios Implementation
subtasks.description: Create tests/e2e simulating full workflows: message → agent → tool approval → resolution across Slack/Discord.
subtasks.details: Use pytest, mock MCP servers minimally. Scenarios: factoid bypass, approval request→button→resolve, timeout deny, multi-thread. Target 10% coverage but critical paths. Run against live connectors if safe.
subtasks.testStrategy: pytest tests/e2e/ --cov=bot --cov-report=html. Verify end-to-end flows succeed, coverage on main paths, error recovery.

subtasks.title: Error Handling and Logging Implementation
subtasks.description: Add try/except blocks in hooks/connectors with user-friendly messages, integrate structlog 24.4.0, suppress stacktraces.
subtasks.details: Wrap async/sync critical paths (API calls, tool execution, approval timeouts). Log structured errors: level='error', event='api_failure'. User messages: 'Service temporarily unavailable, retrying...'. Test error scenarios continue operation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit/integration tests for error paths: assert log emitted, no stacktrace to user, operation continues (e.g., approval timeout → deny).

subtasks.title: Coverage Reporting and Verification
subtasks.description: Configure pytest-cov for combined unit/integration/e2e coverage reports, enforce 80%+ line/75%+ branch thresholds.
subtasks.details: pyproject.toml or pytest.ini: [tool:pytest] addopts = --cov=bot --cov-report=html --cov-fail-under=80. Separate reports: unit 70%, integration 20%, e2e 10%. Generate coverage html for review.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full suite: pytest --cov=bot --cov-report=html --cov-fail-under=80. Verify thresholds met, review html report for gaps in connectors/approval flows.

subtasks.title: Documentation Generation and Update
subtasks.description: Update README.md with setup/install, testing instructions; create architecture.md from PRD covering connectors, approval flow.
subtasks.details: README sections: Quickstart, Testing (pytest commands, coverage), Development. architecture.md: diagrams (mermaid) for agent→hook→connector→approval. Include tool rules config example. Use PRD as source.
